title: Missing Person UPDATE 3: Camille HILDERMAN Located Deceased
content:
Thirty-five year-old Camille HILDERMAN, who was the subject of a missing person investigation, has been located deceased.
HILDERMAN was last seen on Friday, June 5, 2026 in Wolseley, SK. It was believed she had travelled to Regina.
Following several days of investigation and searching, late yesterday afternoon (June 10, 2026), investigators located Hilderman, deceased, near Highway 11 northwest of Regina.
The investigation is now being led by RCMP with the assistance of the Regina Police Service.
Due to this death investigation being in its early stages, no further information is available at this time.
The Regina Police Service would like to thank the public, Regina Fire and Protective Services and the RCMP for their assistance in this ongoing investigation.

title: Request Assistance: Missing 17 Year-old Liberty GARCHINSKI
content:
The Regina Police Service is asking for information from the public that could assist in locating 17 year-old Liberty GARCHINSKI. GARCHINSKI was last seen on June 4, 2026, at approximately 11:30 am in the 1900 block of Broad Street, Regina, Saskatchewan.
Liberty GARCHINSKI is described as: Female, 5’6”, and approximately 120 lbs. with a medium build. GARCHINSKI has Brown hair, and Brown eyes. A photo of GARCHINSKI accompanies this release.
There is no evidence to believe Liberty GARCHINSKI has come to harm, however, they are considered at risk and police would like to locate them to ensure their safety.
Anyone who has information that would help police locate Liberty GARCHINSKI is asked to contact the Regina Police Service at 306-777-6500 or Regina Crime Stoppers at
https://www.reginacrimestoppers.ca/
or 1-800-222-TIPS (8477).
The Regina Police Service thanks the public and the media for their attention to this matter.

title: Investigation Underway Following Discovery of Human Remains
content:
The Regina Police Service, in conjunction with the Saskatchewan Coroners Service, have begun an investigation following the discovery of what appears to be human remains.
On June 9, 2026, at approximately 4:15 p.m., police were dispatched to a call for service on the 1300 block of Retallack Street. Upon arrival, officers located what appeared to be human remains outside a residence. Officers secured the area and requested additional resources from the Regina Police Service Major Crimes Unit, Regina Police Service Forensic Identification Unit and the Saskatchewan Coroners Service.
The death is considered suspicious however, at this early stage of the investigation, there are no further details that will be released to the public.
This investigation is
not
connected to the ongoing investigation of a missing 35 year-old woman.
Anyone who has information that could assist police in this investigation is asked to contact the Regina Police Service at 306-777-6500 or Regina Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477). The Regina Police Service thanks the public and the media for their assistance in this matter.

title: Two Charged Following Break and Enter
content:
An adult male and adult female are facing charges following a break and enter at a residence in the 1200 block of Cameron Street.
At approximately 11:40 p.m. on June 9, 2026, police responded to a report of a break and enter in progress. Officers attended the scene and located the suspects inside the residence. They were taken into custody without incident.
Subsequent to arrest, officers searched the suspects and located brass knuckles and a quantity of drugs. The male suspect was found to be on a conditional release and was in breach of his conditions. A warrant of apprehension was issued as a result.
As a result of this investigation, 19 year-old Andraya BEAR-BIGSKY, is charged with:
Break and Enter with Intent [CC 348(1)(A)]; and
Fail to Comply with Condition of an Undertaking [CC 145(4)(A)].
Twenty-three year-old Blaze LITTLECROW, is charged with:
Break and Enter with Intent [CC 348(1)(A)].
Both made their first appearance on these charges in Regina Provincial Court this morning, June 10, 2026 at 9:30 a.m.

title: UPDATE: Police Operation Concluded – 2000 Blk Elliot St
content:
The Regina Police Service reports the police operation in the 2000 block of Elliot Street is now concluded. The public is free to resume their activities as per normal.
At approximately 9:30 a.m. this morning (June 9, 2026), police were dispatched to a weapons call at a residence in the 2000 block of Elliot Street. Information received indicated a male suspect was outside the residence holding a firearm. Due to the increased risk to public safety, the Regina Police Service SWAT Team and Canine Unit were called in to assist Patrol officers. Several people were taken into custody. The investigation is ongoing and, at this time, no charges have been laid.
The Regina Police Service would like to thank the public for their cooperation.

title: Missing Person UPDATE: Regina Police Service and Regina Fire and Protective Services Conducting Search for 35 Year-Old Camille HILDERMAN
content:
The Regina Police Service (RPS) continues to search for 35 year-old Camille Jeanette HILDERMAN. This afternoon (Tuesday, June 9 ,2026), RPS requested the assistance of Regina Fire and Protective Services to search Lakewood Lake in the city’s northwest end. Residents are asked to avoid the area while the search is carried out.
At this time, officers are focusing their search on the northwest corner of the city and the surrounding rural area immediately outside city limits. This includes the area north of Dalgleish Drive and west of McIntosh Street. Those in this area are asked to check their properties for any sign of HILDERMAN.
HILDERMAN is vulnerable due to a medical condition and there is concern for her well-being.
Camille HILDERMAN was last seen on June 5th, 2026, at approximately 11:00 a.m. in Wolseley, SK. It is believed that she travelled to Regina. HILDERMAN also goes by the name Didi/Dee Dee.
HILDERMAN is described as: female, 5’2”, weighing approximately 104 lbs. HILDERMAN has blonde and pink hair and brown eyes. She has piercings in her nose and lower lip. She has a tattoo on her upper left cheek bone, a triple seven tattoo on her cheek and a ghost on one of her inner arms. She was last seen wearing a black and white ruffled skirt with tights and a white and grey sweater. Photos of HILDERMAN accompany this release.
Anyone who has information that would help police locate Camille HILDERMAN is asked to contact the Regina Police Service at 306-777-6500 or Regina Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477).
The Regina Police Service thanks the public and the media for their attention to this matter.

title: One Charged Following Theft and Assault with a Weapon
content:
At approximately 6:00 p.m. on Sunday, June 7, 2026, police were called to a report of a weapons offence at a business in the 1300 block of Broadway Avenue.
The caller reported an adult male had entered the business, grabbed various grocery items and attempted to leave the store without paying. When employees approached the suspect, he threatened them with a knife before leaving the business on foot.
Officers were able to locate the suspect a short distance away. He was taken into custody without further incident. A search of the suspect, subsequent to arrest, revealed a knife.
As a result, 37 year-old Dustin JURISTA is charged with:
2 x Assault with a Weapon [CC 267(a)];
Shoplifting Under $5,000 [CC 334(b)]; and
Possession of Property Obtained by Crime Under $5,000 [CC 355(b)].
JURISTA will make his first appearance on these charges in Regina Provincial Court on July 20, 2026.

title: WANTED: River PETERS
content:
Wanted: Unlawfully at Large
Thirty-two year-old River PETERS is described as male, 5’7”, approximately 172 lbs. with short black hair. Notable marking include a full neck tattoo, the word “SWK” and a star tattooed on his right forearm, as well as the word “CRY LATER” and a skull also tattooed on his right forearm.
If you have information regarding the whereabouts of PETERS, please leave a tip in the form below (you may choose to leave a tip anonymously), or contact Regina Police Service 306-777-6500 or submit your tip to Regina Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) (online at reginacrimestoppers.ca). Crime Stoppers will pay up to $2,000 for information on this or any other serious crime in Regina. Calls are not traced or recorded and callers do not have to identify themselves.

title: Attempted Murder and Organized Crime Charges Laid
content:
Two adult males are facing charges in relation to an
investigation
that began in mid-April.
At approximately 1:50 a.m. on April 12, 2026, officers were patrolling in the area of Dewdney Avenue and Argyle Street when they observed an injured adult male on the sidewalk. The male was suffering from multiple lacerations. Officers provided first aid until EMS arrived and transported the male victim to hospital to be treated for, what appeared to be, serious injuries.
Investigators with the Regina Police Service Major Crimes Unit, Forensic Identification Unit and Canine Unit were called in to assist Patrol officers. Shortly after 2:00 a.m., the investigation led police to a residence in the 1400 block of Elphinstone Street where a search warrant was executed. Multiple people were located inside the home. Some were taken into custody on outstanding warrants.
Further investigation led police to arrest and charge two adult males in relation to this incident.
On Wednesday, June 10, 2026, 31 year-old Kenneth MANITOPYES was charged with:
Attempted Murder [CC 239]; and
Commission of an Offence for a Criminal Organization [CC 467.12].
MANITOPYES made his first appearance in Regina Provincial Court Thursday, June 11, 2026 at 9:30 a.m.
On Thursday, June 11, 37 year-old Chad KEEWATIN was charged with:
Attempted Murder- [CC 239]; and
Commission of an Offence for a Criminal Organization. [CC 467.12] and
Breach of Release Order [CC145(5)].
KEEWATIN will make his first appearance on these charges in Regina Provincial Court this morning, Friday June 12, 2026 at 9:30 a.m.

title: One Arrested – ICE Investigation – Regina
content:
The Saskatchewan Internet Child Exploitation (ICE) Unit has charged a Regina man with child exploitation-related offences.
On May 14, 2026, the Saskatchewan ICE Unit executed a Search Warrant at a residence in Regina. Electronic devices were examined at the scene and also seized by investigators.
As a result of the investigation, on June 10, 2026, 36-year-old Donavon BLACKSIOUX of Regina was arrested and charged with the following:
Possessing Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material [CC 163.1(4)]; and
Making Available Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material [CC 163.1(3).
BLACKSIOUX made his first appearance on these charges in Regina Provincial Court today, June 11, 2026.
*The Saskatchewan ICE Unit is comprised of investigators from the Saskatchewan RCMP, Regina Police Service and Saskatoon Police Service. Their mandate is to investigate crimes involving Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material.
